column1: # Frontier AI Safety Framework Comparison Dataset

column2: Frontier AI Safety Framework Comparison Dataset (data through 2026-07-12)

column7: Capability thresholds paired with safeguard outcomes; current policy also uses ASL-2 and ASL-3 for maintained protection levels.
column8: Chemical and biological weapons production (non-novel and novel thresholds); Misaligned AI systems in high-stakes settings; Automated R&D in key domains (AI, energy, robotics, weapons development)
column9: Risk Report review can involve one or more external reviewers; v3.4 permits section-level division when all unredacted sections are covered.
column10: Changes are proposed by the CEO and Responsible Scaling Officer and approved by the board in consultation with the Long-Term Benefit Trust; updated versions are published on or before their effective date with differences recorded in a change log.
column11: Nine dated versions through v3.4, with public redlines for recent updates.
column12: Risk Reports and off-cycle model analyses are separate from the framework document; not every model release receives a new Risk Report.
column13: Explicit. Anthropic publishes a separate SB 53 compliance page and the RSP states that statutory definitions are addressed in separate compliance frameworks.
column14: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
column15: Full signatory of the EU GPAI Code of Practice.
column16: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
column17: The RSP's post-v3.0 structure does not map cleanly to its earlier numbered AI Safety Level framing.

column7: Frontier Governance Framework Tier 1/2/3 levels map in part to Preparedness Framework High and Critical thresholds.
column8: Cyber offense; Chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear (CBRN); Harmful manipulation (exploratory); Loss of control / AI self-improvement
column9: The governance framework describes external expert input; the two-document structure must be checked for the exact procedure.
column10: OpenAI commits to a Framework Assessment at least once every 12 months from the effective dates of the California TFAIA and the EU Code of Practice, with material updates documented in a changelog and published within 30 days.
column11: Preparedness Framework has two public major versions; the 2026 Frontier Governance Framework is unversioned on its face.
column12: Preparedness findings and system cards provide model-specific evidence, while the Frontier Governance Framework is the regulatory-facing document.
column13: Explicit. The FGF states it is OpenAI's frontier AI framework under the TFAIA, with OpenAI OpCo LLC responsible for US compliance.
column14: Not named in the FGF as of the 2026-05-28 edition.
column15: Full signatory. The FGF doubles as the public summary of OpenAI's Safety & Security Framework under the EU Code, with OpenAI Ireland Limited the responsible EU provider.
column16: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
column17: OpenAI uses two governing documents with different jobs; treating either as the complete framework loses material context.

column7: Critical Capability Levels for severe risk plus Tracked Capability Levels for earlier, significant-but-less-severe signals.
column8: CBRN uplift; Cyber uplift; Harmful manipulation (exploratory); ML R&D acceleration/automation and misalignment (including stealth and situational awareness)
column9: The framework describes holistic assessments and internal oversight; no universal independent-review requirement was verified for every evaluation.
column10: Reviewed at least once a year, and more frequently if DeepMind has reasonable grounds to believe the framework's adequacy or its adherence has been materially undermined.
column11: Four dated versions from v1.0 through v3.1.
column12: Gemini model cards describe FSF evaluations. The Gemini 3.1 Pro card reports Deep Think below every CCL while cyber crossed an earlier alert threshold.

column14: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
column16: Signatory (Google); framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
column17: TCLs and CCLs are not direct equivalents of another lab's safeguard or preparedness tiers.
